compiled_state.title: Write RED behavioral tests for ticket delete command
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
The `ticket delete` subcommand does not yet exist. These tests define the behavioral contract that T4 must satisfy. All tests must fail RED before T4 is implemented.

## What to do
Create `tests/scripts/test-ticket-delete.sh` following the pattern from `tests/scripts/test-ticket-archived.sh`.

## Files to create
- `tests/scripts/test-ticket-delete.sh` — new RED test suite

## Test functions to implement (one per done definition)

### test_delete_requires_user_approved_flag (DD1)
- Create a ticket
- Run `ticket delete <id>` WITHOUT `--user-approved`
- Assert exit code is non-zero
- Assert stderr/stdout contains usage hint mentioning `--user-approved`

### test_delete_blocked_by_non_deleted_children (DD2)
- Create a parent ticket and one child ticket (link child as child of parent)
- Run `ticket delete <parent-id> --user-approved`
- Assert exit code is non-zero
- Assert output contains the child ticket ID

### test_delete_removes_from_ticket_list (DD3)
- Create a ticket (no children)
- Run `ticket delete <id> --user-approved`
- Assert exit code 0
- Assert `ticket list` output does NOT contain the deleted ticket ID

### test_delete_clears_blocker_for_ready_to_work (DD4)
- Create: ticket A, ticket B (A depends_on B as blocker)
- Verify A is NOT ready_to_work before delete
- Run `ticket delete <B-id> --user-approved`
- Assert `ticket deps <A-id>` JSON has `ready_to_work: true`
- Note: this test requires both T4 (delete command) and T2 (_graph.py fix) to be in place

### test_delete_writes_unlink_events_for_active_links
- Create: ticket A (has a LINK event pointing to ticket B)
- Run `ticket delete <B-id> --user-approved`
- Assert that scanning A's ticket dir finds UNLINK events canceling the active LINK to B

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 tests fail RED before T4 is implemented
- Uses `_make_test_repo()`, `_create_ticket()`, and assert.sh helpers from test library
- Each test is isolated (own temp repo)
- Script is executable (`chmod +x`)

## Testing mode
RED — defining behavioral contract for new ticket delete command

Please provide the ticket details (title, description, acceptance criteria, etc.) so I can analyze what changes are needed and identify the relevant source and test files.
